originalTitle: Apple’s strategy for smart glasses is the same as smart watches
summary: Apple isn't just looking to take on Meta in the smart glasses market; it's looking to upend eyewear as a whole, according to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. When the Apple Watch launched, it wasn't simply competing against the Pebbles and the Motorolas of the world. The company also had Swatch, Fossil, and Seiko in its crosshairs. […]
content: Apple isn’t just looking to take on Meta in the smart glasses market; it’s looking to upend eyewear as a whole, according to Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman. When the Apple Watch launched, it wasn’t simply competing against the Pebbles and the Motorolas of the world. The company also had Swatch, Fossil, and Seiko in its crosshairs. Similarly, Apple’s smart glasses will be positioned not just against Meta and Samsung, but against Oakley, Ray-Ban, and Warby Parker in the $200-$500 range. Apple Watch generates an estimated $17 billion annually, but eyewear is a potentially even bigger prize. Where the watch market is worth an estimated $132 billion, according to Mordor Intelligence, eyewear is estimated to generate between $180 and $200 billion annually. Notably, though, Apple doesn’t plan to play at the highest ends of the market, leaving companies like Cartier and Matsudato to cater to luxury customers. The company tried to compete in the luxury watch market with the $10,000 gold Apple Watch, but it never made much of an impact. So this time around it seems Apple will focus on mainstream consumers. According to Gurman: > The company believes its strong brand, industrial design and iPhone integration will lead people seeking new regular glasses to spring for an Apple pair instead. Apple’s existing ecosystem of over 2 billion active devices, its global retail footprint and the promise of artificial intelligence features that could help people interact with the world around them will help as well.
chineseTitle: 苹果对智能眼镜的策略与智能手表相同
chineseSummary: 苹果不仅仅希望在智能眼镜市场上与 Meta 较量；彭博社的马克·古尔曼 (Mark Gurman) 表示，该公司希望颠覆整个眼镜行业。当 Apple Watch 推出时，它不仅仅是与世界上的 Pebbles 和摩托罗拉竞争。该公司还瞄准了 Swatch、Fossil 和 Seiko。 […]
chineseContent: 苹果不仅想在智能眼镜市场与 Meta 较量，还想在智能眼镜市场上与 Meta 较量。彭博社的马克·古尔曼 (Mark Gurman) 表示，该公司希望颠覆整个眼镜行业。 Apple Watch 推出时，它不仅仅是与世界上的 Pebbles 和摩托罗拉竞争。该公司还瞄准了 Swatch、Fossil 和 Seiko。同样，苹果的智能眼镜不仅会与 Meta 和三星竞争，还会与 200-500 美元范围内的 Oakley、Ray-Ban 和 Warby Parker 竞争。 Apple Watch 每年的收入估计为 170 亿美元，但眼镜的价值可能更大。据 Mordor Intelligence 称，手表市场价值估计为 1,320 亿美元，而眼镜市场每年的产值估计为 180 至 2,000 亿美元。但值得注意的是，苹果并不打算进军高端市场，让卡地亚和松田户等公司只能迎合奢侈品客户。该公司试图通过售价 10,000 美元的金质 Apple Watch 参与豪华手表市场的竞争，但从未产生太大影响。因此，这一次苹果似乎将把重点放在主流消费者身上。 Gurman 表示： > 该公司相信，其强大的品牌、工业设计和 iPhone 集成将引导人们寻找新的普通眼镜，转而购买苹果眼镜。 Apple 现有的由超过 20 亿台活跃设备组成的生态系统、其全球零售足迹以及有助于人们与周围世界互动的人工智能功能的承诺也将有所帮助。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
